title: Lifecycle Dry Run Conformance 2026 04 26
goal: Resolve #378 by making one lifecycle dry-run path plan-shaped and black-box conformance-backed.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Install dry-run exposes a stable lifecycle_plan and conformance proves no fixture writes.
intent.constraints: Do not generate lifecycle mutation adapters, change destructive behavior, or weaken preflight/ownership safeguards.
intent.latitude: Choose one lifecycle path and add the smallest output/conformance contract needed for reviewers to trust the dry-run plan.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Add black-box conformance coverage for one lifecycle dry-run plan path without generating lifecycle mutation commands.
execution.proof: Run generated tool conformance, workspace CLI tests, contract tooling, and root ruff.

invariants: Lifecycle generation remains out of scope.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make lifecycle planning safer to trust before any future lifecycle adapter work.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: agentic-workspace install --dry-run now emits a compact lifecycle_plan with selected modules, planned creates/updates/removals, preserved files, review state, local-only interaction, and next safe command.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: install.lifecycle.dry-run.process conformance passed and proved no writes; workspace CLI tests, contract tooling, and ruff passed.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Add conformance-backed lifecycle dry-run plan coverage for one path.

delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Use install dry-run as the first path and add compact output fields if needed for stable conformance.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.

discard.summary: No broader lifecycle adapter generation was needed for #378.

continuation.summary: #379 remains the next planned lane item.
